retrieved_docs.title: Early Childhood Caries -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: Children with ECC need to be examined in detail. Poor dental health often extends beyond the oral cavity. As caries can affect the child’s mastication and phonetics, the ability to eat, drink, speak, or gain acceptance of peer groups may impact their physical, mental, and psychosocial development.A thorough dental examination is carried out with intraoral periapical radiographs or a panoramic x-ray. Pulp status should also be established to develop an appropriate treatment plan. Clinicians may also require other investigations like a complete blood count (CBC).

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Emergencies -- Evaluation -- Post-procedural Bleeding
retrieved_docs.content: In addition to a thorough physical exam, laboratory studies can be performed preoperative or postoperative if clinically indicated to evaluate hemoglobin and platelet levels, to identify supratherapeutic international normalized ratio (INR) levels with warfarin use and in patients with cirrhosis, and to determine clotting Factor VIII and IX blood levels in patients with Hemophilia A and B, respectively.

retrieved_docs.title: Hyperphosphatemic Tumoral Calcinosis -- History and Physical -- Dental Involvement
retrieved_docs.content: Teeth are involved very commonly in HTC. The various dental defects reported include pulp chamber obliteration, pulp stones, shortening of roots with bulbous enlargement in the middle, and enamel changes. Besides, a peculiar thistle shape of roots has been described. Also, the roots have often been found curved, a process called dilaceration. These dental changes are most commonly seen in premolar teeth. [18] The importance of dental involvement lies in its early recognition by dentists, as often these are the first reported manifestations of HTC before the development of tumoral calcinosis. Thus, appropriate evaluative, preventive, and treatment measures can be initiated once these changes are identified.

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Caries Diagnostic Testing -- Procedures
retrieved_docs.content: Newer technologies in the dental field, including fluorescence, electrical conductance, and lasers, allow professionals to detect caries at an earlier stage. These technologies inform the dentist of the extent of demineralization on a specific tooth and allow for early intervention. Photothermal Radiometry and Modulated Luminescence (PTR-LUM)

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Caries Diagnostic Testing -- Procedures
retrieved_docs.content: PTR-LUM monitors caries-related changes in the tooth's microstructure and can be used adjunctively with radiographs. The measured changes correspond with optical and thermal properties measured by photodetectors and infrared detectors. The resulting PTR-LUM response is viewed on the Canary scale as a number between 1 and 100. A reading between 1 and 20 indicates that the mineralization corresponds with a healthy tooth. A number between 20 and 70 is indicative of demineralization. Lastly, a reading of 70 or greater is a strong indicator of advanced demineralization and decay and requires invasive treatment. [16] Depending on the number, the dental practitioner can determine whether invasive or noninvasive treatment is necessary. Laser Fluorescence Caries Detection Devices

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Caries Diagnostic Testing -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Dental caries remains one of the most widespread chronic infections globally. Fortunately, numerous diagnostic tests developed over the past century now enable earlier detection of the disease, supporting timely intervention and more effective management to address caries before significant progression occurs.

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Fluorosis -- Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: Clinically dental fluorosis can be mistaken for a variety of dental conditions. [31] There is no special investigative test in widespread use that easily differentiates dental fluorosis from other conditions with a similar presentation. [21] Instead, diagnosis is contingent on a positive fluoride history during amelogenesis. [21] The incidence of dental fluorosis is increasing, particularly in Europe and the US. [12]

retrieved_docs.title: Developmental Disturbances of the Teeth, Anomalies of Number -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Management of hyperdontia often involves extraction when the supernumerary tooth contributes to periodontal issues, functional interference, or restorative challenges. If sufficient space exists in the dental arch and no complications arise, observation without intervention remains a valid option. In cases where supernumerary teeth remain impacted and encased within soft tissue or bone, ongoing radiographic monitoring becomes essential to detect potential pathology. When crowding occurs due to the presence of additional teeth, maintaining excellent oral hygiene and attending regular follow-up appointments is crucial for supporting long-term oral health and stability.

retrieved_docs.title: Forensic Odontology -- Issues of Concern -- Responsibilities of forensic odontologist include [3] :
retrieved_docs.content: Identification of human remains Age estimation Identification following mass fatalities Assessing bite mark injuries Assessment abuse cases (child, spousal, elder) Civil cases involving malpractice

retrieved_docs.title: Tooth Fracture -- Deterrence and Patient Education
retrieved_docs.content: Maintaining good dental hygiene and going to regular dental visits help prevent complications by reducing the risk of infection, promoting periodontal health, and ensuring early detection of potential issues. After treatment, patients should be advised to follow proper oral care practices, avoid excessive biting forces on the affected tooth, and adhere to scheduled follow-up visits. Promptly reporting any signs of pain, discoloration, or mobility is essential for early intervention and long-term success.

retrieved_docs.title: Forensic Odontology -- Clinical Significance -- Age Determination Based on Dental Data
retrieved_docs.content: Additionally, advanced methods were developed, which include the practice of using scanning electron microscopes and energy dispersive X-ray analysis ( SEM-EDXA) , an advanced technique to scan dentin for age estimation. Contemporary research from the United Kingdom observed the root length usage in estimating the age of a pediatric individual. Another method developed is known as the Average stage of attrition (ASA) method which is a clinical way of measuring the attrition of molar cusps; it measures the attrition of respective molar cusps and subsequently uses them in average calculations. [10]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 72
retrieved_docs.content: The category “diseases of mouth; excluding dental” is classified as a subcategory of “اِضطِراب الغدة اللُّعابِيَّة” (salivary gland disorder), indicating that non‑dental oral diseases are considered part of the broader spectrum of salivary gland pathology. This relationship highlights the importance of evaluating salivary gland function when assessing oral conditions that are not of dental origin, thereby guiding more comprehensive diagnosis and patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 250
retrieved_docs.content: The extraction of a single tooth is a direct dental procedure performed on the zuby (teeth), involving the removal of one tooth from the oral cavity.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 244
retrieved_docs.content: Extraction of single tooth is classified as a dental procedure, indicating that it has a relationship with the broader category of dental procedures. This connection underscores that the act of extracting a single tooth is a specific type of dental intervention within the overall field of dental procedures.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 249
retrieved_docs.content: Dental procedures are performed directly on zuby mléčné (the primary or “milk” teeth), indicating that therapeutic dental interventions often target the primary dentition. Likewise, disorders of teeth and jaw are located in zuby mléčné, showing that many oral pathologies also affect these early‑life teeth. Together, these relationships emphasize that both clinical treatments and disease processes in pediatric dentistry are closely linked to the primary teeth, underscoring the importance of careful assessment and management of zuby mléčné in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 1 -- Community 226
retrieved_docs.content: Dental procedures are directly performed on zuby mléčné, indicating that primary (baby) teeth serve as the specific sites for these dental interventions.

Recognizing sodium monofluorophosphate as a fluoride source guides oral‑health preventive strategies, while awareness of the SMQ classification aids clinicians in systematic identification and treatment of functional GI disturbances, underscoring the importance of these entities for patient care and product design.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 3 -- Community 2732
retrieved_docs.content: Accidental physical contact (event) is described as being due to loss of teeth caused by an accident, indicating that dental injury resulting from an accident can lead to unintended physical contact.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 33 -- Level 1 -- Community 109
retrieved_docs.content: disorders, and obstetric interventions together form a comprehensive framework for risk assessment, care planning, and management of maternal‑fetal outcomes during pregnancy, delivery, and the postpartum period.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2449
retrieved_docs.content: Sodium phosphate is classified among the drugs used for treatment of hypercalcemia, indicating its role in managing elevated calcium levels.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2179
retrieved_docs.content: Melanomas of skin are malignant neoplasms that arise from melanocytes in the cutaneous tissue and represent a specific form of skin cancer. سرطان الشفة الخبيث, which translates to malignant lip cancer, is a distinct malignant tumor that originates in the lip and is classified as an oral cavity cancer. Although both conditions are cancers, they affect different anatomical sites and are not subtypes of one another; each requires separate diagnostic and therapeutic approaches tailored to its location and biological behavior. Understanding the differences between melanomas of skin and سرطان الشفة الخبيث is important for accurate diagnosis, appropriate treatment planning, and effective patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 4 -- Community 2878
retrieved_docs.content: Diagnostic procedures on the nose, mouth, and pharynx are classified as a subcategory of عمليات الأنف, indicating that these examinations are considered part of the broader group of nasal operations.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2304
retrieved_docs.content: Early, first or unspecified trimester hemorrhage is classified as spotting complicating pregnancy, unspecified trimester.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1494
retrieved_docs.content: A gestational age of 29‑30 weeks completed is associated with a liveborn infant, indicating that pregnancies reaching this stage can result in a live birth.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 1 -- Community 225
retrieved_docs.content: Extraction of single tooth is a direct procedure performed on zuby (teeth), indicating that the teeth are the specific anatomical site for this dental intervention. Disorders of teeth and jaw are described as having zuby as their parent category, meaning that conditions affecting the teeth and jaw are organized under the broader classification of zuby. Consequently, zuby functions both as the procedural target for single‑tooth extraction and as the foundational element within the hierarchy of dental and jaw disorders, underscoring its central importance in dental treatment and pathology.
